title: Bulk report - Week 45
summary:
Capesize
The week began with the major ore routes fixing around US$7.50 for west Australia and US$17.70 for Brazil and currently vessels are achieving around US$8.20 and US$19.50 level respectively, although there is a suggestion that the US$19.50 fixed on Genco Titus for end November dates might have failed. Saldhana Bay to Qingdao was fixed on Koch tonnage for 6-8 December loading at US$14.20 with Anglo. The Genco Tiberius 175,874-dwt 2007-built, open Hong Kong 12-15 November fixed earlier this week for a round voyage via Dalrymple Bay at US$21,000 with Jiangsu Steamship.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: The week began with the major ore routes fixing around US$7.50 for west Australia and US$17.70 for Brazil and currently vessels are achieving around US$8.20 and US$19.50 level respectively, although there is a suggestion that the US$19.50 fixed on Genco Titus for end November dates might have failed. Saldhana Bay to Qingdao was fixed on Koch tonnage for 6-8 December loading at US$14.20 with Anglo.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: The market has slowed and rates have softened everywhere. At the beginning of the week, the Atlantic seemed strong for transatlantic business with a modern kamsarmax fixing at US$16,000 daily for Colombian round voyage.

children.title: Supramax
children.summary: Both basins this week have struggled, with most routes under downward pressure. The only exceptions being from the US Gulf which saw positive gains.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: Once again, another fairly slow week with sentiment lending little support. After a successful private trial, it was the second week that the BHSI 38 index was published basis a public trial, with assessments on the new Imabari 38 benchmark vessel and the seven time-charter routes.
